description: Source of truth for agent tool policy. OpenCode bash permissions in config/opencode/opencode.json must stay in sync with bash_allow, bash_deny, and bash_ask below.

bash_allow.rtk: All rtk subcommands (read, grep, ls, find, git, gh, diff, test, …)
bash_allow.du -s: Parseable disk usage only — not human-oriented dust
bash_allow.du -sh: Parseable disk usage with human suffix
bash_allow.ctx7: Context7 docs lookup (also installs find-docs skill)
bash_allow.githits: Indexed search/grep/read across open-source code (CLI, no MCP)
bash_allow.inf: Inference.net (Catalyst) CLI — gateway, traces, evals, datasets, training
bash_allow.pioneer: Pioneer (Fastino) CLI — datasets, training, evals, inference
bash_allow.bun: Required runtime for the Pioneer CLI
bash_allow.gtimeout: Bound live agent smoke tests on macOS (GNU coreutils timeout)
bash_allow.timeout: Bound live agent smoke tests where GNU timeout is available

redirect.ls: rtk ls (default); rtk tree only when GNU tree is installed (brew install tree)
redirect.eza: rtk ls (default); rtk tree only when GNU tree is installed (brew install tree)
redirect.curl: webfetch or rtk wget when fetching URLs
redirect.gls: rtk ls (default); rtk tree only when GNU tree is installed (brew install tree)
redirect.gsed: fastedit edit or jq/yq for structured data
redirect.gawk: jq/yq for structured data or rtk grep for text
redirect.head: rtk read (rtk output is already condensed — do not pipe through head/tail)
redirect.tail: rtk read (rtk output is already condensed — do not pipe through head/tail)
redirect.python3: fastedit edit (or uv for package ops — not for file writes)
redirect.python: fastedit edit (or uv for package ops — not for file writes)
redirect.echo: jq -n for JSON; rtk test for hook smoke — do not pipe echo to tools
redirect.printf: jq -n for JSON; rtk test for hook smoke — do not pipe echo to tools

skills_installed.tool-policy: House tool policy skill (symlinked from skills/tool-policy/)

policy_no_mcp: We use CLIs + Skills rather than MCP servers. Context7, GitHits, and Inference.net all offer CLI/Skills modes and we use those exclusively.
